2025-26 Los Angeles Clippers Roster and Stats

Previous Season

Record: 42-40, Finished 9th in NBA Western Conference

Coach: Tyronn Lue (42-40)

Executive: Lawrence Frank

PTS/G: 113.8 (24th of 30) Opp PTS/G: 112.6 (10th of 30)

SRS: 0.96 (16th of 30) Pace: 96.5 (28th of 30)

Off Rtg: 117.3 (11th of 30) Def Rtg: 116.1 (18th of 30) Net Rtg: +1.2 (16th of 30)

Expected W-L: 44-38 (16th of 30)

Preseason Odds: Championship +1800, Over-Under 49.5

Arena: Intuit Dome Attendance: 722,504 (20th of 30)

Box Plus/Minus

Box Plus/Minus
A box score estimate of the points per 100 possessions a player contributed above a league-average player, translated to an average team.

Kawhi Leonard 8.0 (5th)
James Harden 3.9 (19th)

Offensive Box Plus/Minus

Offensive Box Plus/Minus
A box score estimate of the offensive points per 100 possessions a player contributed above a league-average player, translated to an average team.

Kawhi Leonard 7.4 (4th)

Defensive Box Plus/Minus

Defensive Box Plus/Minus
A box score estimate of the defensive points per 100 possessions a player contributed above a league-average player, translated to an average team.

Kris Dunn 1.5 (16th)

Value Over Replacement Player

Value over Replacement Player
A box score estimate of the points per 100 TEAM possessions that a player contributed above a replacement-level (-2.0) player, translated to an average team and prorated to an 82-game season.

Multiply by 2.70 to convert to wins over replacement.

Kawhi Leonard 5.3 (5th)
James Harden 3.6 (14th)

Usage Pct

Usage Percentage
An estimate of the percentage of team plays used by a player while they were on the floor.

James Harden 28.6 (17th)

Assist Pct

Assist Percentage
An estimate of the percentage of teammate field goals a player assisted while they were on the floor.

Steal Pct

Steal Percentage
An estimate of the percentage of opponent possessions that end with a steal by the player while they were on the floor.

Kawhi Leonard 2.9 (6th)

Block Pct

Block Percentage
An estimate of the percentage of opponent two-point field goal attempts blocked by the player while they were on the floor.

Turnover Pct

Turnover Percentage
An estimate of turnovers committed per 100 plays.

Kawhi Leonard 8.4 (18th)

Triple-Doubles

Triple-Doubles The NBA did not track Triple-Doubles until 1979-80 when Magic Johnson popularized the feat. Nonetheless, we can present Triple-Doubles since 1950-51 when rebounds became an official statistic. A small number may yet be unaccounted for but we're confident in the completeness of the list.
